title: Vellozia neglecta

This species is native to Brazil and is known to grow in humid and subtropical environments. The classification of Vellozia neglecta places it within the kingdom Plantae. intro:
There is not much information available about Vellozia neglecta. However, I found some information about the Vellozia genus which belongs to the family Velloziaceae.

The plant Vellozia belongs to a genus of flowering plants in the family Velloziaceae, commonly known as "cauliflower plants" due to their unique inflorescences resembling a head of cauliflower. Some species of Vellozia are used in traditional medicine, particularly for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of these plants include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for their medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Vellozia is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures for the treatment of various health issues, including respiratory problems, fever, and digestive disorders.
